title: Sarcomphalus pedunculatus
intro: Sarcomphalus pedunculatus, also known as the "Sarcomphalus" or "Wild Raspberry," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential uses in treating fever and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Sarcomphalus pedunculatus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
taxonomy: Sarcomphalus pedunculatus is classified as a species within the genus Sarcomphalus, which is a part of the family Rhamnaceae. This plant is native to Central and South America. The Rhamnaceae family consists of about 830 species of flowering plants commonly known as buckthorns, and is one of the largest families in the order Rosales.
